title: Reflections on Cylindrical Contact Homology
speaker: Jo Nelson
abstract: This talk beings with a light introduction, including some historical anecdotes to motivate the development of this Floer theoretic machinery for contact manifolds some 25 years ago. I will discuss joint work with Hutchings which constructs nonequivariant and a family Floer equivariant version of contact homology. Both theories are generated by two copies of each Reeb orbit over Z and capture interesting torsion information.

proof_stages.label: Contact and Reeb-orbit preliminaries
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes the basic contact-geometric objects: contact forms, Reeb vector fields, closed Reeb orbits, nondegeneracy, and the elliptic/hyperbolic classification in dimension three.

proof_stages.label: Weinstein-conjecture motivation for SFT
proof_stages.summary: The history of periodic-orbit existence results is used to motivate why pseudoholomorphic-curve machinery in symplectizations led to symplectic field theory and its cylindrical version.

proof_stages.label: Classical cylindrical contact homology complex
proof_stages.summary: Cylindrical contact homology is presented as a Floer-type theory whose generators are good Reeb orbits and whose differential counts asymptotically cylindrical pseudoholomorphic cylinders with appropriate multiplicity weights.

proof_stages.label: Hypotheses and prior invariance results
proof_stages.summary: The expected chain-complex and invariance statements are restricted by low-index contractible orbits, leading to the hyper-tight and dynamically convex regimes and a survey of known geometric and abstract-perturbation results.

proof_stages.label: Transversality and compactness obstruction
proof_stages.summary: Multiply covered curves and holomorphic-plane bubbling are identified as the main obstruction to a direct autonomous-J definition, especially for cobordism maps and invariance.

proof_stages.label: Nonequivariant check-hat cascade complex
proof_stages.summary: Domain-dependent almost complex structures force a nonequivariant Morse-Bott-style enlargement with check and hat generators, point-constrained cascade moduli spaces, and a matrix differential which is stated to be well-defined, square-zero, and invariant under suitable hypotheses.

proof_stages.label: Obstruction-bundle gluing correction
proof_stages.summary: The difficult d-minus corner is corrected by analyzing branched covers of trivial cylinders glued to planes, reducing the relevant gluing count in the dynamically convex three-dimensional case to a winding number of asymptotic leading coefficients.

proof_stages.label: Equivariant Borel construction and recovery of EGH theory
proof_stages.summary: A Borel-type S^1-equivariant construction is built from the nonequivariant theory, and after tensoring with Q it is canonically identified with the original Eliashberg-Givental-Hofer cylindrical contact homology when the latter is defined, yielding its invariance.

examples.name: irrational ellipsoid test case for obstruction-bundle gluing
examples.note: The simple ellipsoid shows that the d-minus coefficient would give the wrong homology without the obstruction-bundle gluing contribution, thereby motivating the correction term.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker uses the irrational ellipsoid as a concrete obstruction-bundle-gluing test case, specifying the relevant iterates, index difference, plane foliation input, and the ±1 obstruction contribution

passes.reason: The toric irrational ellipsoid has extremally minimal Reeb dynamics and is used to show that the obstruction-bundle-gluing term cannot be omitted.
